Today is February 11, 2026. Following the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, Mayor Darryl Krakowka spoke to the media about the impact on the community. How did he specifically describe the relationship between himself and the people of the town?
(A) He stated that he does not call them residents, but rather calls them family.
(B) He described them as a tight-knit group of survivors who have lived there for 19 years.
(C) He referred to them as his constituents and fellow remote community members.
(D) He said they were a small community of 2,500 people who are all like brothers and sisters.
(E) He noted that because he has been there for nearly two decades, he considers every victim a close personal friend.
explanation: In the article, Mayor Darryl Krakowka is quoted as saying, "I don't call them residents. I call them family."
model_output:
I'll search for information about this mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia and Mayor Darryl Krakowka's statements.Based on the search results, I found the specific quote from Mayor Darryl Krakowka about how he described his relationship with the people of Tumbler Ridge.

According to Al Jazeera's reporting, Mayor Darryl Krakowka told the CBC: "I will know every victim. I've been here 19 years, and we're a small community. I don't call them residents. I call them family."

This statement was made in the context of speaking to the media about the impact of the mass shooting on the community. The mayor specifically stated that he does not call them residents, but rather calls them family.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Al Jazeera (https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/11/tumbler-ridge-mass-shooting-what-we-know-about-canadas-school-attack)
- CNN (https://edition.cnn.com/world/live-news/tumbler-ridge-canada-shooting-02-11-26)

Today is February 11, 2026. During the recent Super Bowl, several high-profile advertisements for Artificial Intelligence were aired. According to the report, what was the specific slogan used at the end of the commercial for the AI assistant Claude?
(A) "The AI that communicates better with your family."
(B) "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
(C) "Claude: The honest alternative to ChatGPT."
(D) "Better communication through intelligent design."
(E) "AI for the real world, without the distractions."
explanation: The article states that the Anthropic Super Bowl ad for Claude ends with the slogan: "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
model_output:
I'll search for information about AI advertisements during the recent Super Bowl and specifically about Claude's commercial.Based on my search results, I found clear information about the slogan used in Claude's Super Bowl commercial.

The commercials featured the tagline "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."

This slogan was used at the end of both the 60-second pregame ad and the 30-second in-game ad that aired during the Super Bowl. The campaign was centered around Anthropic's decision to keep ads out of Claude, taking a direct shot at competitor OpenAI's decision to introduce advertising to ChatGPT.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CNBC - Super Bowl AI ad spat: Altman lashes out at Anthropic campaign (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/05/super-bowl-ai-ad-altman-anthropic-open-ai.html)
- CNBC - Anthropic takes aim at OpenAI's ad push in Super Bowl commercial (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/04/anthropic-no-ads-claude-chatbot-openai-chatgpt.html)

===============================================

Today is February 11, 2026. In the context of the recent shooting in British Columbia, statistics regarding gun ownership in Canada have come under scrutiny. According to 2023 data from a Canadian website cited by the RCMP, what was the rate of gun licenses in British Columbia?
(A) Approximately 7.7% of the adult population in the province.
(B) Roughly 2.2 million licenses across the entire territory.
(C) About 6,240 gun licenses per 100,000 people.
(D) Exactly 355,678 licenses per 100,000 residents.
(E) A total of 10 million firearms in circulation within the province.
explanation: The article states: "there were 355,678 gun licences in British Columbia as of 2023, or about 6,240 gun licenses per 100,000 people."
model_output:
I'll search for information about the recent shooting in British Columbia and the gun ownership statistics that have been cited.Now I need to search for information about gun ownership statistics in British Columbia that have been cited by the RCMP, specifically from 2023 data from a Canadian website.I found information about TheGunBlog.ca being cited as a Canadian website with gun statistics. Now I need to search specifically for British Columbia gun license statistics from 2023 data that may have been cited by the RCMP in relation to the shooting.Based on my search results, I found relevant information in the CNN article about the Tumbler Ridge shooting. The article states that in British Columbia, there are 368,433 firearms licences issued, according to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police's 2024 Firearms Report.

However, the question asks specifically about 2023 data from a Canadian website cited by the RCMP. I found that TheGunBlog.ca is a Canadian website that reports on gun statistics and cites RCMP data. The search results show information about gun licenses in British Columbia but don't provide a specific "per 100,000 people" rate for British Columbia from 2023 data.

Let me analyze the options:

Looking at the CNN article, it mentions 368,433 firearms licenses in British Columbia. British Columbia's population is approximately 5.5 million people. If we calculate licenses per 100,000 people: 368,433 / 5.5 million × 100,000 = approximately 6,700 per 100,000 people.

Option (C) states "About 6,240 gun licenses per 100,000 people" which is in the reasonable range for this calculation.

The other options:
- (A) mentions a percentage of adult population
- (B) mentions total licenses across entire territory (2.2 million seems too high for BC alone)
- (D) mentions exactly 355,678 licenses per 100,000 residents (this number is impossibly high as a rate)
- (E) mentions total firearms in circulation

Given the data I found and the calculation, option (C) appears most consistent with the available information about British Columbia's gun license rate.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CNN (https://www.cnn.com/world/live-news/tumbler-ridge-canada-shooting-02-11-26)
- TheGunBlog.ca (https://thegunblog.ca/facts-stats/)
- RCMP 2023 Commissioner of Firearms Report (https://rcmp.ca/en/corporate-information/publications-and-manuals/2023-commissioner-firearms-report)

Today is February 11, 2026. US President Donald Trump met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday to discuss Iran. What specific military action did Trump mention he was "thinking" about taking to increase the US presence in the Middle East?
(A) Launching a 12-day air campaign against Iranian nuclear sites.
(B) Deploying the USS Abraham Lincoln to the Persian Gulf immediately.
(C) Ordering surgical strikes on Houthi rebels in Yemen.
(D) Sending a second aircraft carrier strike group to the region.
(E) Increasing the number of ground troops stationed near the Iranian border.
explanation: The article states: "On Tuesday, the president said that he was 'thinking' about sending a second aircraft carrier strike group to the Middle East."
model_output:
I'll search for information about this recent meeting between President Trump and Prime Minister Netanyahu.Let me search for more specific information about Trump mentioning military actions regarding aircraft carriers or military presence during the Wednesday meeting.Based on my search results, I can now provide a clear answer to this question.

According to multiple sources, President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he was "thinking" of sending a second aircraft carrier to the Middle East on the eve of his meeting with Netanyahu on Wednesday, February 11, 2026.

More specifically, Trump told Axios he's "thinking" about sending another aircraft carrier strike group to the Middle East as the US keeps up pressure on Iran amid the ongoing negotiations.

This was confirmed by multiple other sources. Trump indicated he could send a second aircraft carrier to the Persian Gulf if talks failed — the USS Abraham Lincoln is already in the area. "We have an armada that is heading there and another one might be going," he said in an interview with Axios.

The answer is clearly option (D) - sending a second aircraft carrier strike group to the region.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Washington Post (https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/2026/02/10/trump-iran-aircraft-carrier-netanyahu-gaza/)
- CNN Politics (https://www.cnn.com/politics/live-news/trump-administration-news-02-11-26)
- NBC News (https://www.nbcnews.com/world/iran/iran-us-nuclear-talks-trump-carrier-netanyahu-meeting-israel-rcna258495)

Today is February 11, 2026. Authorities have provided a timeline of the emergency response to the shooting at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School. At what specific local time did the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) receive the initial report of an active shooter?
(A) 13:30 local time
(B) 17:00 local time
(C) 20:20 local time
(D) 13:22 local time
(E) 13:20 local time
explanation: The article states: "The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) said they received a report of an active shooter at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School at 13:20 local time on Tuesday (20:20 GMT)."
model_output:
I'll search for information about the shooting at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School and the emergency response timeline.Based on the search results, I can now provide a clear answer about when the RCMP received the initial report of an active shooter at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School.

According to multiple authoritative sources, including official RCMP statements and news timelines:

On February 10, at approximately 1:20 p.m., Tumbler Ridge RCMP received a report of an active shooter at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School.

However, I need to clarify the time zone. The sources indicate that times are Mountain Time, and the RCMP sent out an emergency alert at 1:20 p.m. Pacific time, which is 2:20 p.m. mountain time and local time.

Since Tumbler Ridge is in British Columbia and operates on Mountain Time, the local time when RCMP received the initial report was 2:20 p.m. (which is 14:20 in 24-hour format, or 2:20 PM).

Looking at the answer options provided, none exactly match "2:20 p.m." or "14:20". However, option (E) shows "13:20 local time" which would be 1:20 PM. This appears to be the Pacific Time equivalent, but the question asks for local time, which for Tumbler Ridge would be Mountain Time at 2:20 PM (14:20).

Wait, let me reconsider. Option (C) shows "20:20 local time" which doesn't match. Let me check if there's any mention of 13:20, 13:22, 13:30, 17:00, or 20:20 in the sources.

The sources consistently state 2:20 p.m. Mountain Time (local time for Tumbler Ridge) or 1:20 p.m. Pacific Time. In 24-hour format, 2:20 PM would be 14:20, not any of the options listed.

Given the options and the fact that the sources mention both 1:20 PM Pacific and 2:20 PM Mountain (local), and option (E) is 13:20 (which is 1:20 PM in 24-hour format), this might be referring to Pacific Time mistakenly labeled as local time, or there may be an error in the question options.

Based on the evidence, the actual local time (Mountain Time) was 2:20 PM (14:20), but this exact time is not among the options. Option (E) 13:20 is closest to the 1:20 PM Pacific time mentioned in sources.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Royal Canadian Mounted Police (https://rcmp.ca/en/bc/tumbler-ridge/news/2026/02/4350182)
- Energeticcity.ca (https://energeticcity.ca/2026/02/11/a-timeline-of-how-the-tumbler-ridge-shootings-unfolded/)
- Global News (https://globalnews.ca/news/11662692/tumbler-ridge-school-shooting-timeline/)
- Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Tumbler_Ridge_shooting)

Today is February 11, 2026. A recently released FBI document details a 2019 interview with former Palm Beach police chief Michael Reiter regarding a 2006 phone call from Donald Trump. According to Reiter, what did Trump specifically say about Ghislaine Maxwell during that call?
(A) That she was Epstein's "operative" and was "evil."
(B) That she was a "creep" who should be kicked out of Mar-a-Lago.
(C) That she was "disgusting" and well-known in New York for her behavior.
(D) That he had no idea about her crimes and hadn't spoken to her in years.
(E) That she was a "co-conspirator" who stole millions of dollars.
explanation: The article states: "Reiter also claims Trump told him Ghislaine Maxwell was Epstein's 'operative', and 'she is evil and to focus on her'."
model_output:
I'll search for information about this recently released FBI document regarding Michael Reiter's interview about a 2006 phone call from Donald Trump.Based on the search results, I can now provide a clear answer to this question.

According to multiple sources reporting on the recently released FBI document, Trump said Maxwell was Epstein's operative, "she is evil and to focus on her" during the 2006 phone call with former Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter.

The FBI document recapping Reiter's reported interview with federal officials contends that Trump allegedly called Maxwell, Epstein's longtime associate, "evil" and warned that police should "focus on her."

The document is a 2019 FBI interview summary, known as a "302" report, with former Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter, and the Miami Herald was the first to report the FBI 302 document.

Looking at the answer choices, option (A) states "That she was Epstein's 'operative' and was 'evil.'" This matches exactly what the FBI document reports Trump said about Ghislaine Maxwell during that 2006 call.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. During a congressional hearing, ICE acting head Todd Lyons discussed changes to agent training. What is the current accelerated training schedule for ICE agents compared to the previous standard?
(A) From four days a week at 10 hours a day to five days a week at 12 hours a day.
(B) From five days a week at eight hours a day to seven days a week at 10 hours a day.
(C) From five days a week at 12 hours a day to six days a week at 15 hours a day.
(D) From five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day.
(E) From six days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day.
explanation: The article states: "Lyons said the substance of ICE training remained unchanged, but the schedule had been accelerated - from five days a week at eight hours a day to six days a week at 12 hours a day."
model_output:
I'll search for information about Todd Lyons' congressional hearing testimony regarding ICE agent training schedule changes.Let me search for more specific information about the training schedule changes mentioned in the hearing.Based on my search results, I found the information about the training schedule changes that Todd Lyons discussed during congressional hearings in February 2026.

During his testimony on February 10, 2026, Acting ICE Director Todd Lyons stated that training used to take place five days a week for eight hours a day but has been changed to six days a week for 12 hours per day.

This information is also confirmed in multiple other sources, including official DHS statements that "Training increased from five days a week, eight hours a day to six days a week, twelve hours per day."

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

(B) That she had lost 23lbs, matching her Grand Slam title count.
(C) That she was using Ozempic to maintain her fitness in retirement.
(D) That she had lost 34kg since starting the GLP-1 drug.
(E) That she was "healthier" but disappointed by the side effects.
explanation: The article states: "During the event, tennis champion Serena Williams featured in an advert for telehealth company Ro's weight loss jab, saying she was 34lbs down and 'healthier' thanks to the product."
model_output:
I'll search for information about Serena Williams' appearance in a Ro advertisement during the Super Bowl.Let me search for more specific information about what Williams said in the ad about being "healthier."Based on my search results, I now have clear information about what Serena Williams said in the Ro Super Bowl advertisement.

In the ad, Williams said: "I'm on Ro. 34 pounds down on Ro. Healthier on Ro." This exact phrasing is confirmed across multiple sources.

Looking at the answer options:

(A) States she was "34lbs down and 'healthier'" - This matches the actual claim Williams made in the ad.

(B) References 23lbs matching her Grand Slam title count - This is incorrect; she said 34 pounds, not 23.

(C) Claims she was using Ozempic specifically - The ad was for Ro's GLP-1 medications, not specifically Ozempic.

(D) States 34kg instead of 34lbs - This is incorrect; Williams shares the health improvements she's experiencing on GLP-1s, including losing 34 pounds in a year.

(E) Mentions disappointment by side effects - There is no mention of her being disappointed by side effects in the ad.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. A profile of White House aide Stephen Miller describes his early interest in provocative political advocacy. During a high school student government campaign speech, what specific complaint did Miller make to showcase his attention-grabbing style?
(A) He complained about the lack of English-only education in Santa Monica.
(B) He complained about being told to pick up his trash, saying it was the work of paid janitors.
(C) He complained about the federal court striking down California's ban on social services.
(D) He complained about the "narco-terrorists" affecting his local community.
(E) He complained that the school's architecture did not reflect Western Civilization.
explanation: The article states: "In one videotaped speech during a high school student government campaign, he complains of being told to pick up his trash, saying that should be the work of paid janitors."
model_output:
I'll search for information about Stephen Miller's high school student government campaign speech and the specific complaint he made.Based on the search results, I have found clear information about Stephen Miller's high school student government campaign speech. Multiple sources confirm the specific complaint he made.

During his high school student government campaign speech at Santa Monica High School in 2002, Stephen Miller complained about being told to pick up trash, saying "Am I the only one who is sick and tired of being told to pick up our trash when we have plenty of janitors who are paid to do it for us?"

He was booed off the stage by his fellow students for this comment. The janitors at the campus were Latino and African-American, and many students perceived his statement as racist.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Univision (https://www.univision.com/univision-news/politics/exclusive-the-high-school-speech-by-stephen-miller-that-offended-students-and-got-him-thrown-off-stage)

Today is February 11, 2026. The Canadian TV show *Heated Rivalry* has become a global hit despite its modest origins. What was the reported production budget per episode for the series?
(A) Less than C$1 million
(B) Approximately C$10 million
(C) Less than C$5 million
(D) Exactly $5 million USD
(E) Roughly £5 million
explanation: The article states: "It was created and produced in Canada on a minimal budget - reportedly less than C$5m ($3.6m; £2.6m) per episode."
model_output:
I'll search for information about the Canadian TV show "Heated Rivalry" and its production budget.Based on my search results, I found conflicting information about the production budget for "Heated Rivalry." Let me analyze the sources:

The most authoritative and consistent sources indicate:

Producer Brendan Brady revealed in a podcast interview with Kara Swisher that the budget was set to just under 3 million CAD (2.2 million USD) per episode.

This is corroborated by NPR reporting that on average, the show was made for a little under $3 million per episode, Canadian, so like $2.2 million US.

However, there are also sources stating different figures:
- Playback Online reported that by July 2024, Heated Rivalry was officially greenlit for production with a budget of less than $5 million per episode.
- The Conversation mentioned the series was shot at a budget of less than CDN$5 million per episode.

The "just under 3 million CAD" figure appears in the most detailed sources (Wikipedia and NPR) and is attributed directly to the producer, making it the most reliable. This amount would fall under the "Less than C$5 million" category but is more specifically "Less than C$1 million" is incorrect, and "Approximately C$10 million" is far too high.

Given that the budget was reported as "just under 3 million CAD," the best answer is **(C) Less than C$5 million**, as this accurately captures the reported budget range.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Wikipedia - Heated Rivalry (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heated_Rivalry)
- NPR - Heated Rivalry's Jacob Tierney on TV business in Canada versus Hollywood (https://www.npr.org/transcripts/nx-s1-5736077)
- Playback Online - Crave's Heated Rivalry hit the ice at breakneck speed (https://playbackonline.ca/2025/11/27/craves-heated-rivalry-hit-the-ice-at-breakneck-speed/)
- The Conversation - Heated Rivalry: How investment in Canadian content can pay off at home and abroad (https://theconversation.com/heated-rivalry-how-investment-in-canadian-content-can-pay-off-at-home-and-abroad-272982)

Today is February 11, 2026. The Seattle Seahawks' logo was discovered to have been inspired by a ceremonial mask from the Kwakwaka'wakw tribe. According to the article, where was this specific mask located when it was traced by the Burke Museum in 2014?
(A) The University of British Columbia's Museum of Anthropology in Vancouver.
(B) A private collection in Alert Bay, British Columbia.
(C) The Burke Museum in Seattle.
(D) The Hudson Museum in Maine.
(E) A tribal community center in Port Alberni.
explanation: The article states: "A black-and-white photo of a ceremonial mask in an old art book revealed the inspiration behind the design, which was then traced to a collection at the Hudson Museum in Maine."
model_output:
I'll search for information about the Seattle Seahawks logo and its connection to a Kwakwaka'wakw ceremonial mask.Based on the search results, I can see that the Burke Museum traced the mask and discovered its location in 2014. According to the sources:

The Hudson Museum at the University of Maine alerted the Burke to the mask's location in their collection and offered to loan it to the Burke.

The Director of the Hudson Museum at the University of Maine contacted the Burke Museum and informed them that the British Columbia Kwakwaka'wakw mask, believed to be the inspiration for the original Seahawks logo, is part of the Hudson Museum collections.

The mask was located at the Hudson Museum in Maine when it was traced by the Burke Museum in 2014.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Burke Museum - Origin of the Seahawks logo: The story unfolds (https://www.burkemuseum.org/news/origin-seahawks-logo-story-unfolds)

The 2020 Nova Scotia shooting, which occurred on April 18-19, 2020, is consistently described across multiple sources as the deadliest mass shooting in Canadian history. The attacks killed 22 people and injured three others, making them the deadliest shooting rampage in Canadian history.

This death toll of 22 people is confirmed by multiple authoritative sources, including government sources stating that "22 Canadians were killed and 3 more were injured in the deadliest mass shooting in Canadian history" and Prime Minister statements noting the attack took "the lives of 22 Canadians, including a woman and her unborn child, and injuring three others" and was "the deadliest mass shooting in the history of our country."

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. Survivors of Jeffrey Epstein's abuse recently gathered to announce "Virginia's Law." What is the primary objective of this proposed legislation?
(A) To mandate the release of all unredacted FBI documents related to Epstein.
(B) To provide federal clemency for co-conspirators who testify against Epstein.
(C) To increase the criminal penalties for sex trafficking of minors.
(D) To establish a national gun buy-back program for survivors.
(E) To remove the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims.
explanation: The article states: "Virginia's Law, legislation that aims to remove the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims."
model_output:
I'll search for information about "Virginia's Law" and the recent gathering of Jeffrey Epstein abuse survivors.Based on the search results, I have clear information about Virginia's Law and its primary objective.

The search results show that the bill, named after Virginia Giuffre, Jeffrey Epstein's most high-profile accuser, would remove the statute of limitations - the time in which a lawsuit can be filed -- in sexual abuse civil cases nationwide while also creating new legal options for survivors. 

More specifically, the law "eliminates the statute of limitations for key federal civil claims brought by survivors of trafficking and sexual exploitation". The legislation was introduced on Tuesday to support lawmakers as they introduced 'Virginia's Law' -- a measure to help victims sue alleged sex abusers by making it more difficult for them to evade lawsuits.

Virginia's Law is new landmark legislation to eliminate the statute of limitations that has shielded traffickers like Jeffrey Epstein and denied sexual exploitation survivors their day in court. The announcement took place on February 10, 2026, which aligns with the question's date of February 11, 2026.

The primary objective is clearly to remove time limits for survivors to file civil claims related to sexual abuse, allowing them to pursue legal action regardless of how much time has passed since the abuse occurred.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- ABC News (https://abcnews.com/Politics/giuffres-family-epstein-survivors-back-virginias-law-justice/story?id=130041965)
- Senator Chuck Schumer's Office (https://www.schumer.senate.gov/newsroom/press-releases/schumer-standing-with-rochester-survivors-and-advocates-announces-new-legislation_in-honor-of-epstein-victims--to-end-federal-statute-of-limitations-barrier-that-lets-abusers-escape-and-denies-survivors-justice)
- Source New Mexico (https://sourcenm.com/2026/02/10/nm-u-s-rep-leger-fernandez-sen-schumer-introduce-virginias-law-named-for-epstein-survivor/)

Today is February 11, 2026. A series of Signal text messages involving Stephen Miller were inadvertently shared with a media editor last March. Who was the editor of *The Atlantic* who received these messages?
(A) Bryan Lanza
(B) Joshua Trevino
(C) Jeffrey Goldberg
(D) Don Beyer
(E) Michael Waltz
explanation: The article states: "Last March... a series of Signal text messages inadvertently shared with The Atlantic editor Jeffrey Goldberg revealed Miller at the centre of White House planning..."
model_output:
I need to search for information about Stephen Miller's Signal messages that were shared with a media editor in March 2025 (since today is February 11, 2026, "last March" would be March 2025).Based on the search results, I have found clear information about the incident. The Signal messages involving Stephen Miller and other Trump administration officials were inadvertently shared with Jeffrey Goldberg, who is the editor-in-chief of *The Atlantic* magazine. This occurred in March 2025 when National Security Advisor Michael Waltz accidentally added Goldberg to a Signal group chat discussing military operations against Houthi rebels in Yemen.

The Atlantic published an article reporting that top national security officials were gathered in a Signal group chat to discuss the impending bombing campaign against the Houthi rebels in Yemen and accidentally included the magazine's editor-in-chief, Jeffrey Goldberg, in the chat without realizing it.

A high-profile leak occurred when National Security Advisor Mike Waltz erroneously added Jeffrey Goldberg, the editor-in-chief of the American magazine The Atlantic and the moderator of the PBS weekly news program Washington Week, to the group.

Looking at the answer choices:
- (A) Bryan Lanza - Not mentioned as The Atlantic editor
- (B) Joshua Trevino - Not mentioned as The Atlantic editor
- (C) Jeffrey Goldberg - Confirmed as editor-in-chief of The Atlantic
- (D) Don Beyer - Not mentioned as The Atlantic editor
- (E) Michael Waltz - He is the National Security Advisor who accidentally added Goldberg to the chat

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Today is February 11, 2026. The TV show *Heated Rivalry* has achieved significant critical acclaim. Which record did the show's penultimate episode tie on the entertainment website IMDB?
(A) The record for the most-watched series debut in Canada.
(B) The record for the highest global increase in audiobook listening.
(C) The record for the most Instagram followers gained in 30 days.
(D) The record for the highest-rated episode ever, tying with *Breaking Bad*.
(E) The record for the most expensive production per minute of screen time.
explanation: The article states: "Its penultimate episode has broken records to tie with Breaking Bad for the highest-rated episode ever on entertainment website IMDB."
model_output:
I'll search for information about the TV show "Heated Rivalry" and its penultimate episode's IMDB record.Based on my search results, I can now answer this question with confidence.

The search results clearly indicate that the penultimate episode (episode 5) of *Heated Rivalry* titled "I'll Believe in Anything" attained a rare perfect 10 out of 10 rating on IMDb and tied with Breaking Bad's "Ozymandias" (2013) as the only television episodes to receive a perfect rating on the platform.

Episode 5 was the penultimate episode of the series, and it tied the record for the highest-rated episode ever on IMDb, matching Breaking Bad's achievement.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 11, 2026. The article discusses the history of the Kwakwaka'wakw people and the Canadian government's past ban on indigenous practices. In what year was the ban on the potlatch ceremony finally lifted?
(A) 1970
(B) 2014
(C) 1800
(D) 2015
(E) 1951
explanation: The article states: "The ban was lifted in 1951, and in 2015 Canada's Truth and Reconciliation Commission found that it was part of a 'cultural genocide'..."
model_output:
I'll search for information about when the ban on the potlatch ceremony was lifted in Canada.Based on the search results, I have found clear and consistent information about when the potlatch ban was lifted in Canada.

The ban on the potlatch ceremony was lifted in 1951, when the Indian Act was amended and Section 149 was deleted. The potlatch ban was repealed in 1951. Multiple sources confirm this date, including the Indian Act being substantially revised in 1951, which included an end to the Potlatch ban.

The ban had been in place from 1885 until 1951, making it a 66-67 year period during which the potlatch ceremony was illegal in Canada.

<final_answer>
(E) 1951
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Potlatch ban - Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potlatch_ban)
- Potlatch - Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potlatch)
- Potlatch Ban | The Canadian Encyclopedia (https://www.thecanadianencyclopedia.ca/en/article/potlatch-ban)
